case_number: constitutional petition by contending that no quota

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, LARKANA Constt:Petition.No.D-508 of 2015. _________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF HON’BLE JUDGE ___________________________________________________ Before: Mr. Justice Irshad Ali Shah, Mr. Justice Shamsuddin Abbasi, For hearing of main case. 14.12.2021 Mr. Faiz Muhammad Larik, Advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Abdul Hamid Bhurgri, Addl.A.G Sindh. = * = * = * = * = * = It is contended by learned counsel for the petitioner that the petitioner is son of retired employee of Public Health Engineering Department; therefore, he is entitled to be appointed in Public Health Engineering Department under son-quota. Learned Addl.A.G Sindh has sought for dismissal of the instant constitutional petition by contending that no quota is reserved for appointment in public service for sons of retired employees. We have considered the above arguments and perused record. No provision of law is referred by learned counsel for the petitioner, which could have made the petitioner entitled to be appointed in public service other than the merits only for the reason that his father is a retired public servant. Consequently, the instant constitutional petition being devoid of merits is dismissed accordingly. J U D G E J U D G E
